claim: Solomon Islands coalition politics are notoriously fluid; the leader of a no-confidence motion does not always become PM, as coalition negotiations can produce compromise candidates.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands coalition politics are notoriously fluid; the leader of a no-confidence motion does not always become PM, as coalition negotiations can produce compromise candidates.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, after a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Rick Hou, the opposition coalition elected Manasseh Sogavare as PM, not the motion's leader.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare was elected prime minister by parliament on 24 April 2019, replacing Rick Hou, after a no-confidence vote.

cases.description: In 2014, after the general election, a coalition led by the Democratic Alliance Party elected Manasseh Sogavare as PM, bypassing other candidates.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare was elected prime minister by parliament on 9 December 2014, heading a coalition government.

claim: Solomon Islands' shift to China in 2019 and subsequent tensions with Taiwan create external pressure on leadership choices, affecting coalition stability.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands' shift to China in 2019 and subsequent tensions with Taiwan create external pressure on leadership choices, affecting coalition stability.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare switched diplomatic recognition from Taiwan to China, leading to internal political turmoil and a no-confidence vote.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands switches diplomatic recognition from Taiwan to China, prompting political crisis.

claim: Solomon Islands relies heavily on foreign aid, particularly from China and Australia, which influences political alignments and coalition formation.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands relies heavily on foreign aid, particularly from China and Australia, which influences political alignments and coalition formation.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2021, the Solomon Islands government accepted a security pact with China, reflecting aid-driven alignment.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands signs security pact with China, deepening ties amid aid dependence.
